description_long: This object is a vintage-style corded telephone with a solid, angular base and a matching green handset resting on top. The base is trapezoidal, tapering upward, and features a prominent rotary dial on the front, with circular finger holes and a central label area. The handset is broad and rectangular, with pronounced earpiece and mouthpiece sections, and sits securely in a cradle integrated into the top of the base. The phone is uniformly colored in a glossy green finish, with the rotary dial and label area in contrasting black and off-white tones. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, reminiscent of mid-20th-century telephones commonly found in homes and offices. No visible coiled cord connects the handset to the base, and the phone appears to be made primarily of hard plastic with some internal metal components.
description: A vintage green corded telephone with a rotary dial and a matching handset resting on a sturdy, angular base. The phone features a glossy finish and classic design elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, label area, and the handset resting on the cradle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the angular shape of the base and the profile of the handset, with the rotary dial visible on the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid back of the base and the full width of the handset, with no visible controls or cords.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the base's angular lines and the handset's profile, with the rotary dial on the right.
